_meta.purpose: Machine-readable term map consumed by Tier-1 adapters and Tier-2 templates. Documents LangGraph both as a conversion TARGET (idioms to generate) and as a SOURCE (constructs to map into the neutral IR). Values are the authoritative LangGraph equivalents.

StateGraph.import: from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END
StateGraph.pattern:
builder = StateGraph(State)
builder.add_node('a', a)
builder.add_edge(START, 'a')
graph = builder.compile()
StateGraph.notes: The core primitive: a graph you build yourself. Use for any source with named steps, branching, loops, HITL, or custom state. Do NOT collapse a multi-node graph into a single create_react_agent.

CompiledGraph.notes: The runnable returned by builder.compile(); exposes .invoke/.stream/.ainvoke/.astream. Pass checkpointer=... and/or store=... to compile().

ReactAgent.notes: Prebuilt graph for a single tool-calling ReAct agent. Only use when the source truly is one agent with no bespoke control flow.

TypedDict.langgraph_equivalent: TypedDict / Pydantic BaseModel / dataclass state schema
TypedDict.notes: State is a schema passed to StateGraph(State). Nodes receive the full state and return a PARTIAL dict of changed keys. Pydantic BaseModel adds runtime validation.
TypedDict.pattern:
class State(TypedDict):
    topic: str
    score: float
    messages: Annotated[list, add_messages]

MessagesState.notes: Prebuilt TypedDict with a single messages: Annotated[list, add_messages] field. Subclass to add your own keys.

Annotated[list, add].langgraph_equivalent: Annotated[list, operator.add] (or Annotated[list, add_messages] for chat)
Annotated[list, add].import:
import operator
from langgraph.graph.message import add_messages
Annotated[list, add].notes: A reducer. Nodes return only the DELTA; the reducer concatenates it onto the existing value. No reducer means last-write-wins (replace).
Annotated[list, add].pattern:
audit_log: Annotated[list[dict], operator.add]
messages: Annotated[list, add_messages]

reducer_return_rule.notes: For a reducer key, return the new items only (e.g. {'audit_log': [one_entry]}), NEVER the whole accumulated list. Returning the full list double-appends every step.

node_function.signature: def node(state: State) -> dict   (or async def; optional second config arg)
node_function.import: # plain function; add via builder.add_node('name', node)
node_function.return_convention: Return a PARTIAL dict of ONLY the keys this node changed. Do NOT return the whole state object or a bare value. Keys omitted are left unchanged.
node_function.anti_pattern: return state  # returning the whole mutated state object

add_node.langgraph_api: builder.add_node('name', node_fn)  # or builder.add_node(node_fn) to infer name
add_node.notes: Nodes are referenced by string name in edges.

command_node.notes: A node that both updates state and picks the next hop. With goto set you do not need static out-edges. Primary tool for multi-agent handoffs.
command_node.pattern:
def supervisor(state) -> Command[Literal['researcher', '__end__']]:
    return Command(update={'log': [x]}, goto='researcher')

add_edge.notes: Unconditional edge. Nodes referenced by string name. Use START/END sentinels for entry/terminal.

add_conditional_edges.notes: router_fn(state) returns a node name (or a key mapped by the optional dict). May return END, or a list of names to fan out. Mapping dict is optional.
add_conditional_edges.pattern:
def route(s):
    if s['passed']: return 'approve'
    return 'retry' if s['retries'] < MAX else 'drop'
builder.add_conditional_edges('validate', route, {'approve': 'approve', 'retry': 'gap_gen', 'drop': 'drop'})

cycle_loop.source_pattern: graph with a cycle (node A -> node B -> node A)
cycle_loop.langgraph_pattern: back-edge plus a conditional edge whose guard is the iteration cap
cycle_loop.pattern:
builder.add_edge('gap_gen', 'validate')
builder.add_conditional_edges('validate',
    lambda s: 'gap_gen' if (not s['passed'] and s['retries'] < MAX) else 'done',
    {'gap_gen': 'gap_gen', 'done': END})
cycle_loop.notes: Always include a termination guard. recursion_limit in the invoke config is a backstop; hitting it raises GraphRecursionError.

fan_out_send.langgraph_api: return [Send('worker', substate) for x in items]
fan_out_send.notes: Dynamic parallel map-reduce dispatch from a conditional edge. Multiple static out-edges also run in parallel; updates merge via reducers at the join.

@tool.registration: Bind to a model with llm.bind_tools([...]) and/or execute via ToolNode([...]).
@tool.notes: Signature + docstring become the tool schema. Do not emit dead tool-wrapper classes.

bind_tools.notes: Attaches tool schemas to the chat model so it can emit tool calls on the AIMessage.

ToolNode.notes: Prebuilt node that runs the tool calls on the last AIMessage and appends ToolMessages. Pair with tools_condition router and a 'tools' -> 'model' back-edge to form the ReAct loop.

interrupt.langgraph_equivalent: interrupt(payload) inside a node; resume with Command(resume=value)
interrupt.requires: A checkpointer + config={'configurable': {'thread_id': ...}}. Without them the interrupt cannot resume.
interrupt.pattern_resume:
result = graph.invoke(inputs, config)
if '__interrupt__' in result:
    payload = result['__interrupt__'][0].value
    decision = present_to_human(payload)
    result = graph.invoke(Command(resume=decision), config)
interrupt.static_interrupt: graph = builder.compile(checkpointer=saver, interrupt_before=['approve'])  # or interrupt_after=[...]; resume with graph.invoke(None, config); optionally graph.update_state(config, {...}) first
interrupt.notes: Always keep an automated fast-path. Never implement HITL as auto-approve-only with real logic commented out.

MemorySaver.persistent_imports:
from langgraph.checkpoint.sqlite import SqliteSaver
from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
MemorySaver.notes: Saves state per superstep keyed by thread_id. Every stateful invoke MUST pass the thread_id config. Inspect/rewind with get_state / get_state_history / update_state. SqliteSaver/PostgresSaver are context managers or need .setup().

Store.pattern:
graph = builder.compile(checkpointer=MemorySaver(), store=InMemoryStore())
# in a node: store.put((ns,), key, value); store.get((ns,), key)
Store.notes: Checkpointer = per-thread state. Store = memory shared across threads.

graph.invoke.notes: Runs to completion, returns the final state dict.

graph.stream.langgraph_equivalent: for chunk in graph.stream(state, config, stream_mode='updates'): ...
graph.stream.notes: stream_mode: 'values' (full state), 'updates' (per-node deltas), 'messages' (LLM tokens), 'custom'. Pass a list to combine.

graph.astream.langgraph_equivalent: async for chunk in graph.astream(state, config, stream_mode='messages'): ...

providers.notes: LangGraph is model-agnostic; inject a LangChain chat model. Swap the constructor to change provider; the graph is unchanged. .bind_tools works on any tool-calling chat model.

init_chat_model.notes: Provider-agnostic factory; selects the integration from the model string.

reject_list.description: Any generated output containing the following is a conversion error when LangGraph is the TARGET.
items: node returning the whole mutated state object instead of a partial dict
returning the full accumulated list for a reducer key instead of the delta
router function defined but never wired into add_conditional_edges
collapsing a multi-node graph into a single create_react_agent call
interrupt( without a checkpointer + thread_id config
HITL as auto-approve-only with real logic in comments
compile(checkpointer=...) then invoking without thread_id config
cycle with no termination guard / iteration cap
stdlib module in requirements.txt (json, os, io, operator, typing, dataclasses, functools)
